numeric_tests.question: Transaction-value distributions differ by Category
numeric_tests.interpretation_rule: Distributional association only; not a causal group effect.

numeric_tests.question: Transaction-value distributions differ by Location
numeric_tests.interpretation_rule: Distributional association only; not a causal group effect.

numeric_tests.question: Transaction-value distributions differ by Payment Method
numeric_tests.interpretation_rule: Distributional association only; not a causal group effect.

numeric_tests.question: Transaction-value distributions differ by Discount Applied
numeric_tests.interpretation_rule: Distributional association only; not a causal group effect.

numeric_tests.question: Known recorded discount groups: Yes versus No
numeric_tests.interpretation_rule: Descriptive only; discount amount and net-sales semantics are unavailable.

numeric_tests.question: Unit price is monotonically associated with quantity
numeric_tests.interpretation_rule: Association only; price is not a randomized treatment.

numeric_tests.question: Complete-month recorded sales show a monotonic time trend
numeric_tests.interpretation_rule: A non-significant monotonic test does not rule out seasonality or nonlinear change.

categorical_tests.question: Missing sales is associated with category
Missing sales is associated with channel
Missing sales is associated with payment method

cluster_aware_estimate.estimand: Mean recorded transaction value: Online minus In-store
cluster_aware_estimate.caution: Only 25 customer clusters are available, so uncertainty remains fragile.

multiple_testing: Benjamini-Hochberg FDR across the reported core test family
global_cautions: Rows are repeated observations from only 25 customers and are not fully independent.
P-values are interpreted with effect sizes and are not causal evidence.
Weak observed missingness associations do not prove MCAR.
Recorded discounts cannot be evaluated as treatment effects without discount amount and net-sales definitions.
